studies.title: IMPACT — Observational Study on Immunoadsorption (IA) in Patients With Autoantibody-Positive Post-Infectious ME/CFS
studies.location: Charité - Universitätsmedizin Berlin, Institute for Medical Immunology, Berlin

studies.title: MDC002 Phase 1 Clinical Trial in ME/CFS (planned)

papers.title: Imbalance of Excitatory and Inhibitory Neurotransmitter Systems in Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
papers.summary: Reviews evidence for broad neurotransmitter imbalance (glutamate/GABA, adrenergic, serotonin, dopamine) in ME/CFS and post-COVID, linking ionic dysregulation in muscle to central nervous system symptoms including brain fog, hypervigilance, and 'wired but tired'.
papers.why: By Wirth and Scheibenbogen — connects the skeletal-muscle calcium/sodium hypothesis to neurological symptoms, broadening the mechanistic rationale for MDC002 and autoantibody-targeting therapies.

papers.title: Charting the circulating proteome in ME/CFS using cross-system profiling to uncover mechanistic insights
papers.authors: Hoel A, Hoel F, Dyrstad SE, Chapola H, Rekeland IG, Risa K, Alme K, Sørland K, Brokstad KA, Marti HP, Mella O, Fluge Ø, Tronstad KJ
papers.summary: Aptamer-based proteomics of 7,326 targets in 50 ME/CFS patients vs 29 controls identified 845 significantly different proteins; intracellular skeletal-muscle proteins were reduced, while coagulation, complement, and cytokine proteins were elevated, pointing to immune dysregulation and tissue hypoperfusion.
papers.why: Largest serum proteomics study in ME/CFS; provides a molecular map linking muscle pathology and immune activation, supporting both the Wirth/Scheibenbogen hypothesis and autoimmune therapy targets.

papers.title: Proteomic signatures in cerebrospinal fluid and their clinical associations in patients with ME/CFS
papers.authors: Bragée B, Li P, Meadows D, et al.
papers.summary: Quantified 902 CSF proteins in 31 ME/CFS patients; identified enrichment of neutrophil degranulation and platelet activation in POTS-positive patients, and complement/coagulation pathways in severe disease.
papers.why: First large CSF proteomics study in ME/CFS; links POTS, disease severity, and neuroinflammation to specific protein signatures potentially useful as biomarkers for stratifying patients in drug trials.

papers.title: Pathophysiological mechanisms of post-exertional malaise: an integrative analysis based on the metabolism-immune-neuro interaction model
papers.authors: Jin H, An Y, Huang J, Luo T, Wu X
papers.summary: Proposes a three-system model of PEM: mitochondrial dysfunction generates oxidative stress → activates immune inflammasome (IL-1β, TNF-α) → drives neuroinflammation that crosses the blood-brain barrier, forming a self-perpetuating cycle.
papers.why: Integrative framework explaining why mild exertion triggers disproportionate ME/CFS relapse; supports mitochondrial and anti-inflammatory drug targets including MDC002's mechanism.

papers.title: Elevated serum levels of interleukin-11 and matrix metalloproteinase-9 in myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome
papers.authors: Chinnappan B, Kempuraj D, Aenlle KK, Middleton A, Day KS, Kothuru SP, Joshi RS, Klimas NG, Theoharides TC
papers.summary: Serum IL-11 and MMP-9 are significantly elevated in ME/CFS patients compared to controls; EBV-stimulated mast cells release high MMP-9, implicating mast-cell activation and EBV in ME/CFS pathogenesis.
papers.why: Identifies IL-11 and MMP-9 as potential blood biomarkers for ME/CFS and links EBV-triggered mast-cell activation to disease mechanism, opening new therapeutic targets.

papers.title: Large-scale investigation confirms TRPM3 ion channel dysfunction in Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
papers.authors: Sasso EM, Er TS, Eaton-Fitch N, Hool L, Muraki K, Marshall-Gradisnik S
papers.summary: Multi-site study confirms significant reduction in TRPM3 ion channel function in NK cells from ME/CFS patients, replicated across two independent laboratories, establishing TRPM3 as a consistent and reproducible biomarker.
papers.why: Validates TRPM3 as a diagnostic biomarker for ME/CFS; TRPM3 dysfunction in NK cells may underlie immune dysregulation and is a potential drug target.

papers.title: Plasma cell targeting with the anti-CD38 antibody daratumumab in myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome — a clinical pilot study
papers.authors: Fluge Ø, Rekeland IG, Sørland K, Alme K, Risa K, Bruland O, Tronstad KJ, Mella O
papers.summary: Pilot trial of subcutaneous daratumumab in 10 female ME/CFS patients; 6/10 improved, 5 had sustained remission at 12-24 months; SF-36 physical function rose from 25.9 to 55.0; IgG decreased 48%.
papers.why: First published pilot trial of plasma cell depletion in ME/CFS; striking response rate and durability support the autoantibody hypothesis and lay the groundwork for a randomised Phase 2 trial announced by Fluge.

papers.title: Safety, tolerability and clinical effects of rovunaptabin (BC007) on fatigue and quality of life in patients with Post-COVID syndrome (reCOVer): a phase IIa randomised controlled trial
papers.authors: Hohberger B, Ganslmayer M, Harrer T, et al.
papers.summary: First placebo-controlled RCT of rovunaptabin (BC007) in 30 post-COVID patients with GPCR autoantibodies; significant improvements in FACIT fatigue, Bell score, FSS, and quality-of-life vs placebo; neutralised GPCR autoantibodies in all treated patients.
papers.why: First positive RCT for an autoantibody-neutralising agent in post-COVID/ME-like disease; directly supports Scheibenbogen's GPCR autoantibody hypothesis and trials pipeline for immunoadsorption and BC007.

papers.title: Key Pathophysiological Role of Skeletal Muscle Disturbance in Post COVID and Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS): Accumulated Evidence
papers.journal: Journal of Cachexia, Sarcopenia and Muscle
papers.summary: Review synthesising evidence that mitochondrial damage in skeletal muscle — caused by sodium/calcium overload triggered by hypoperfusion — is the central pathological driver of ME/CFS and post-COVID. Presents electron microscopy and MRI sodium data from biopsies.
papers.why: By Scheibenbogen and Wirth; foundational mechanistic basis for Mitodicure's MDC002 (Na⁺/K⁺-pump stimulation) and for autoantibody-mediated vascular targets; key reference for MDC002 development rationale.
